title: Fix SwarmDetailView to auto-inject current swarm ID when creating law tokens

requirements.summary: Auto-inject current swarm ID into CreateLawTokenDialog to prevent 'Parent Swarm does not exist' error
acceptanceCriteria: Clicking the Yes or No plus button on a swarm's detail view opens the CreateLawTokenDialog without triggering 'Parent Swarm does not exist' error
The current swarm's ID is automatically injected into the law token creation call without any user input
A law token is successfully created and associated with the correct parent swarm under the Yes or No location
After successful creation, the UI remains on the swarm detail view page
The new law token appears in the correct Yes or No panel of the swarm detail view after creation

file_operations.description: Modify the SwarmDetailView component to pass the current swarm's ID (from the swarmId URL parameter or component state) into the CreateLawTokenDialog when the Yes or No plus button is clicked. Ensure the swarm ID is captured from the view's own state/props and passed as a prop to CreateLawTokenDialog, so that when the dialog calls actor.createLocation, it uses this pre-populated swarm ID as the parentSwarmId argument. This eliminates the 'Parent Swarm does not exist' error by ensuring the correct swarm context is always provided.

Modify the CreateLawTokenDialog component to accept a new optional prop parentSwarmId of type NodeId (string). When this prop is provided, use it directly as the parentSwarmId argument in the actor.createLocation call instead of leaving it undefined or requiring user input. Ensure the dialog does not prompt the user to confirm or enter the swarm ID when it is pre-populated. Update the mutation call to use the provided parentSwarmId, ensuring the backend receives the correct swarm context for law token creation.
